_meta.notes: applied_job_history_signal: Ava's existing Google Sheet has companies she has already applied to — use this list to DOWN-SCORE duplicate opportunities so the scout surfaces NEW companies and roles she hasn't touched yet.

highlights: Directed audience engagement and cross-functional production for theatrical productions

boost_for: Job is in entertainment, media, theater, talent, or arts
Company is a talent agency or casting company
Role involves marketing, PR, partnerships, or events
Role involves client or partner relationship management
Job description mentions Airtable, CRM, or similar tools she knows
Job is at a company known for a strong creative culture
Job involves live events, productions, or audience engagement
Entry-level or coordinator-level position with growth potential
penalize_for: Job requires specialized technical skills she lacks (engineering, finance, law)
Food / beverage / hospitality industry with no entertainment angle
Pure sales role with quota/commission focus
Role explicitly says 'Manager' in the title (already filtered upstream)
scoring_guidance.context_note: Ava is a May 2025 NYU graduate. She is applying to entry-level roles in NYC. She wants to work in entertainment, media, or the arts. Her strongest asset is a combination of administrative precision + marketing/PR communication skills + live events experience. The ideal job sits at the intersection of entertainment and communications.

applied_job_history_note: The user's Google Sheet contains jobs Ava has already applied to, including hyperlinks. Use this as a signal: SLIGHTLY DOWN-SCORE jobs at companies she has already applied to in the current scrape run, since seeing the same company twice adds no new opportunity. Do not eliminate them entirely — a second role at a company she applied to in a different department is still valid.
